prompt: Write two sentences describing how it feels when someone asks you the same thing six different ways hoping for a different answer.
response: By the fifth repetition, my patience has worn thinner than a dollar store paper towel, each nearly identical query grinding against my already stated response like sandpaper on raw nerves. I am now a human echo chamber, silently screaming into the void of redundant communication, wondering if rephrasing the same question incrementally will somehow magically transform my original, unchanged answer.
